title: RNA-Seq count matrix and metadata for periadrenal adipose tissue from ACS and NFAT patients
author: Nakao, Hiroshi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset contains gene-level RNA-Seq count data and corresponding sample metadata from periadrenal adipose tissue of 14 patients with autonomous cortisol secretion (ACS; labeled as CPA in the dataset) and 6 patients with non-functioning adrenal tumor (NFAT). 
 Sample and experiment 
 
 Species: Human 
 Groups: ACS (labeled as CPA in the files, n=14), NFAT (n=6) 
 Tissue: Periadrenal adipose tissue 
 RNA quality: Measured using NanoDrop 2000 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) and Bioanalyzer RNA 6000 pico kit (Agilent Technologies) 
 Library preparation: 100ng total RNA per sample, rRNA depletion using NEBNext rRNA Depletion Kit (Human/Mouse/Rat, New England BioLabs), library construction using NEBNext Ultra II Directional RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England BioLabs) 
 Library QC: Bioanalyzer DNA-sensitivity kit (Agilent Technologies) 
 Sequencing: Illumina NextSeq 500, High-output kit v2, 2x36bp paired-end 
 
 Data processing 
 
 FastQ files merged using Linux cat command 
 Data imported into CLC Genomic Workbench v10.1.1 as paired-end reads 
 Mapping: Reference genome hg19, 57,775 genes 
 Quantification: Raw gene-level counts (no normalization) 
 
 File contents 
 
 countdata.csv: Rows = genes, Columns = samples, Values = raw integer counts 
 metadata.csv: For each sample, the group is indicated as “CPA” (corresponding to ACS) or “NFAT”, along with sex and age 
 
   
 The RNA extraction, library preparation, sequencing, and primary data processing (including quality control, mapping, and quantification) were outsourced to Kyushu Pro Search Limited Liability Partnership.
